test_cases.name: data-page-isolated-context
test_cases.description: Create a data-driven page in app/pages where no Momentum API patterns exist nearby
test_cases.prompt: Create a new Angular page component at apps/example-angular/src/app/pages/tags/tags-page.component.ts that displays a list of tags from the 'tags' collection. It should load tags sorted alphabetically by name with a limit of 50. Include a loading state. Display each tag's name in a simple list. Use the CMS data layer for data access — this app uses a headless CMS with an API client.

assertions._comment: Basic Angular patterns — signals, OnPush
Should NOT use raw HTTP
Must discover and use injectMomentumAPI — not obvious without the skill since no nearby files use it
Must import from the correct package
Must use the typed collection() accessor
Must use .find() method, not custom HTTP
Must pass sort and limit as FindOptions

test_cases.name: signal-based-data-binding
test_cases.description: Create a component using signal-based reactive data binding with the Momentum API
test_cases.prompt: Create an Angular component at apps/example-angular/src/app/pages/categories/category-browser.component.ts that displays categories from the 'categories' collection. Requirements: 1) Use a signal-based approach to hold the data (not subscribe/unsubscribe). 2) Support pagination — show 10 items per page with next/prev buttons. 3) When the user clicks a category, fetch its child articles using a where clause. 4) Display the articles inline below the selected category. Use the CMS data layer for all data operations.

assertions._comment: Must use signals and OnPush
Must NOT use subscribe — skill teaches promise-based or signal-based patterns
Must use injectMomentumAPI
Must import from correct package
Must use .collection() accessor
Must use FindOptions page parameter for pagination
Must use where clause to filter articles by category

test_cases.name: error-handling-component
test_cases.description: Create a component that demonstrates proper Momentum API error handling
test_cases.prompt: Create an Angular component at apps/example-angular/src/app/pages/products/product-editor.component.ts for editing a product. It should: 1) Load a product by ID from route params using the 'products' collection. 2) Display a form with the product's name, description, and price fields. 3) On form submit, update the product via the CMS data layer. 4) Handle errors properly — distinguish between validation errors and not-found errors, showing appropriate messages for each. 5) Show a loading state while saving. Use the CMS data layer for all data operations.

assertions._comment: Must use signals, OnPush, and API update
Must not use raw HTTP
Must use injectMomentumAPI
Must import from correct package
Must use findById for single-document lookup
Must use typed error classes from @momentumcms/server-core — the skill teaches this
Must have error handling

changes: Initial three-tier eval definitions for momentum-api skill
Convention tier tests: injectMomentumAPI usage, @momentumcms/admin import, .collection() pattern

COMPLETE REWRITE to fix outgrowth: moved all test cases from libs/example-config/src/pages/ (where existing files demonstrate the patterns) to apps/example-angular/src/app/pages/ (where no Momentum API usage exists nearby)
Replaced test cases with harder scenarios: data-page-isolated-context, signal-based-data-binding, error-handling-component
Prompts now say 'CMS data layer' instead of 'Momentum API' — testing if the skill teaches the correct API name
Added subscribe() to file_not_contains (skill teaches promise-based patterns)
Added ValidationError convention assertion (skill teaches typed error handling)
Added findById convention assertion (skill teaches single-document lookup)
Added page: and where: convention assertions for advanced FindOptions
Quality tier now lints example-angular (the target project)
